major_candidate.candidate_title: Context-Invariant Recoverability and Shared Augmentation Threshold Package

core_definitions.conditioned_exactness: Each context admits an exact decoder, potentially context-specific.
core_definitions.context_invariant_exactness: A single decoder recovers the target across all contexts.
core_definitions.context_invariance_gap: Maximum context residual under the best shared decoder (0 iff invariant exactness).
core_definitions.shared_augmentation_threshold: Minimal number of shared rows needed to restore context-invariant exactness.

theorem_candidates.statement: There exist finite restricted-linear families where conditioned exactness holds while context-invariant exactness fails.
No classifier using only context-count and rank descriptors can decide context-invariant exactness on the canonical scaling family.
theorem_candidates.witness: descriptor-matched exact and fail context pairs in both causal and Willems families

theorem_candidates.statement: Shared augmentation threshold can be strictly positive even when conditioned exactness already holds.
theorem_candidates.witness: minimal_shared_rows=1 in both causal and Willems canonical pairs

no_go_candidates.statement: Conditioned exactness does not imply context-invariant exactness.
Stacked single-context exactness is insufficient to certify context-invariant exactness.

validated_regime_results: same-rank observational vs interventional opposite verdict in causal lane
same-sample same-rank opposite verdict in Willems lane
one-row shared augmentation threshold in both selected lanes
new_relative_to_repo: explicit multi-context exactness split: conditioned vs context-invariant
context-invariance gap as a branch-level diagnostic above count/rank summaries
shared augmentation threshold law tied to multi-context compatibility
likely_reframing_only: global fiber/factorization logic still underlies the new package
some statements can be rewritten as lifted linear compatibility constraints

attack_1_implied_by_existing_factorizati.test: Translate invariant requirement to existing fiber language and check whether claims are purely renaming.
attack_1_implied_by_existing_factorizati.details: Core logic is compatible with fiber/factorization foundations, but the conditioned-vs-invariant split and shared threshold are not explicit in the current theorem package.

attack_2_reduces_to_single_rowspace_incl.test: Check stacked row-space exactness on fail contexts.
attack_2_reduces_to_single_rowspace_incl.details: Both stacked checks are exact while context-invariant exactness fails, so stacked single-context criteria alone are insufficient.

attack_3_small_family_overfit.test: Extend fail families with additional scaled contexts and retest invariant exactness.
attack_3_small_family_overfit.details: Adding additional scaled contexts preserves conditioned exactness and invariant failure in the canonical family.

attack_4_descriptor_choice_fragility.details: Exact and fail families share the same rank/count descriptor yet split on invariant exactness.

attack_5_application_only_no_math.test: Require theorem/no-go statements with explicit witnesses and proof sketches.
attack_5_application_only_no_math.details: Three branch-limited proved-on-family statements were extracted; broader generalization remains open.

translation.admissible_family: environment-indexed linear families of latent causes/confounders
translation.exactness: single decoder recovers target across all environments
translation.impossibility: cross-environment decoder incompatibility despite per-environment exactness
translation.augmentation: shared intervention/measurement row that restores invariance

deep_claims.statement: Same-rank observational and interventional measurements can produce opposite exactness verdicts.
deep_claims.evidence: observational rank=1 exact=false vs interventional rank=1 exact=true

deep_claims.statement: Conditioned exactness (each context exact) does not imply context-invariant exactness (single shared decoder).
deep_claims.evidence: contexts [1,0] and [2,0] each recover target [1,0], but no common scalar decoder exists

deep_claims.statement: A one-row shared intervention can collapse context-invariant failure to exactness in the canonical causal family.

translation.admissible_family: distributed terminal observation maps with fixed total terminal budget
translation.disturbance: terminal allocation mismatch and blind terminal directions
translation.compatibility: joint row-space compatibility of terminal bundle
translation.exactness: global decoder for all protected coordinates
translation.impossibility: terminal arrangement yields mixed fibers despite same total rank
translation.augmentation: add terminal channel aligned with missing protected direction

translation.admissible_family: trajectory libraries indexed by experiment context
translation.target: state/output functionals to reconstruct from data
translation.disturbance: poor excitation directionality and context scaling mismatch
translation.compatibility: shared decoder consistency across data contexts
translation.exactness: single map from records to target across experiment contexts
translation.impossibility: same data volume and same rank but incompatible directional support
translation.augmentation: shared excitation row improving context-invariant richness

deep_claims.statement: Same sample budget and same observation rank can still split into exact and impossible recoverability in trajectory-data settings.
deep_claims.evidence: H_exact rank=2 exact=true, H_fail rank=2 exact=false, both with 4 rows

deep_claims.statement: Per-context exactness of data maps does not imply context-invariant exactness for a shared decoder.
deep_claims.evidence: context pair I and diag(2,1) for target I is individually exact but jointly incompatible under one decoder

deep_claims.statement: A one-row shared excitation augmentation restores invariant exactness in the canonical two-context data family.

translation.exactness: exact parameter distinguishability in chosen model
translation.augmentation: basis redesign rather than record count increase

translation.admissible_family: sheet-profile MHD-like fields parameterized by width
translation.exactness: closure-consistent regime with low concentration defects
translation.impossibility: concentrated defects where closure mismatch dominates
